mechanism_narrative: FEDFUNDS has held constant at 5.25% for three consecutive months after a prolonged tightening cycle ending mid-2006. A sustained pause following multi-quarter hikes signals the Fed believes policy is sufficiently restrictive, reducing forward discount-rate uncertainty and supporting equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS unchanged at 5.25% for three consecutive readings as of 2006-09
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS-pause duration and forward 20d SPX return is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns following pauses are not significantly higher than baseline (p>0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling more than 10% below their trailing 12-month average signal a contraction in residential investment demand that propagates to consumer wealth effects and construction employment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% below its trailing 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1795k units, down sharply from 2005 peaks near 2100k
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in more than 55% of instances where the HOUST threshold trigger fires, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains elevated relative to CPIAUCSL-implied inflation (a high real policy rate) while INDPRO growth simultaneously decelerates, the joint condition signals restrictive monetary policy colliding with weakening industrial output, raising recession risk and depressing equity forward returns over the next quarter. Neither series alone captures this stagflation-adjacent risk; the interaction of tight real rates and slowing production is required.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS minus trailing 12-month CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 2pp AND INDPRO 3-month annualized growth turns negative
regime_hint: restrictive real-rate regime with decelerating industrial output
failure_condition: Joint trigger fires but 60d forward SPX returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC (<0.02) versus either single-series proxy.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime with UNRATE near cyclical lows (4.7%), further declines in UNRATE reflect labor market tightness that raises wage-cost pressure and inflation risk, which the market interprets negatively rather than positively, reversing the typical countercyclical relationship seen earlier in expansions and producing negative forward S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE below 5.0% while payroll growth (PAYEMS) remains positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7%, near multi-year lows as of 2006-08
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: During periods with UNRATE below 5.0%, forward 20d SPX returns are positive in more than 55% of instances, contradicting the hypothesized late-cycle reversal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects resilient consumer spending, which is the largest component of GDP demand, and this momentum carries forward into short-horizon equity returns as earnings expectations are revised upward over the following week.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 368,233 million dollars, trending upward through mid-2006
regime_hint: any regime with positive consumer spending momentum
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS 3-month momentum and forward 5d SPX return falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerating below a 1% trailing 3-month annualized pace signals weakening manufacturing supply-side momentum that precedes broader economic slowdown, leading market participants to price in lower forward earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO trailing 3-month annualized growth rate falls below 1%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 113.7063 as of 2006-08, growth rate moderating from earlier in the year
failure_condition: When trigger fires, forward 20d SPX returns are non-negative in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint Sahm-rule-like deterioration where UNRATE rises by 0.3pp or more from its 3-month low while PAYEMS growth simultaneously turns negative signals an emerging labor market inflection that historically precedes broad economic contractions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter. Neither the unemployment rise nor the payroll decline alone reliably signals this inflection; their co-occurrence is required.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >=0.3pp from its trailing 3-month low AND PAYEMS month-over-month change turns negative
regime_hint: labor market inflection at business cycle peak
failure_condition: Joint trigger fires historically but forward 60d SPX returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or interaction adds no incremental Rank IC versus either single series alone.

mechanism_narrative: The negative equity impact of declining housing starts (HOUST) is amplified specifically during monetary tightening regimes because restrictive FEDFUNDS levels compound the credit-cost drag on housing demand; in easing or neutral regimes the same HOUST decline has muted or reversed equity impact because falling rates offset the housing slowdown.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines for 3+ consecutive months while FEDFUNDS remains at or above 5.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.25% concurrent with declining HOUST as of 2006-09
regime_hint: monetary tightening or plateau regime coincident with housing downturn
failure_condition: The negative HOUST-return relationship shows no significant difference (t-test p>0.10) between high-FEDFUNDS and low-FEDFUNDS regimes, indicating the regime conditioning adds no explanatory power.
